old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-ntx4","title":"Ack.to_bytes uses fixed bitmap encoding that may not match RFC 8724","description":"File: python/src/lichen/schc/fragment.py:105-119

Ack.to_bytes() encodes the ACK as:
  byte1 = ((self.window & 1) << _W_SHIFT) | (0x01 if self.complete else 0)
  ...
  body = (bits << pad).to_bytes((n + pad) // 8, 'big') if n else b''
  return bytes([self.rule_id, byte1, n]) + body

This format is:
  [rule_id][W|flags][bitmap_len][bitmap...]

However, RFC 8724 section 8.3.3 specifies the ACK format differently depending on the mode. For ACK-on-Error with 1-bit window:
  [rule_id][W][C][bitmap (if C=0)]

Where:
- W is 1 bit
- C is 1 bit (indicates completion)
- Bitmap follows only if C=0 (incomplete)

The current implementation:
1. Uses a full byte for W+flags instead of bit-packing
2. Always sends the bitmap length explicitly
3. Sends bitmap even when complete=True

This may cause interoperability issues with strict RFC 8724 implementations.

Fix: Verify the ACK format against RFC 8724 section 8.3.3 and adjust encoding accordingly.

Category: PROTOCOL","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:30:47.895897Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:30:47.895938Z"}

new_value: Updated Ack.to_bytes to omit n+bitmap on complete=True per RFC 8724 §8.3.2; from_bytes now handles 2-byte complete ACKs. Tests pass including roundtrip and reassembly.
